title: Programming and DS - DS - Binary Tree

questions.question:
If the number of leaves in a strictly binary tree is an odd number, then what can you say with full conviction about total number of nodes in the tree ?
(A) It is an odd number.
(B) It is an even number.
(C) It cannot be equal to the number of leaves.
(D) It is always greater than twice the number of leaves.


Data Structures: UGC NET CSE | December 2009 | Part 2 | Question: 21

In a full binary tree of height k, there are ______ internal nodes .



Data Structures: UGC NET CSE | June 2009 | Part 2 | Question: 27

The Inorder traversal of the tree will yield a sorted listing of elements of tree in



Data Structures: UGC NET CSE | June 2012 | Part 2 | Question: 27

A binary search tree is a binary tree in which



Data Structures: UGC NET CSE | June 2012 | Part 2 | Question: 8
options: All items in the left subtree are less than root
All items in the right subtree are greater than or equal to root
Each subtree is itself a binary search tree

questions.question:
A complete \( n \)-ary tree is a tree in which each node has \( n \) children or no children. Let \( I \) be the number of internal nodes and \( L \) be the number of leaves in a complete \( n \)-ary tree. If \( L=41 \), and \( I=10 \), what is the value of \( n \)?



Data Structures: UGC NET CSE | October 2020 | Part 2 | Question: 23

The min. number of nodes in a binary tree of depth d (root at level 0) is



Data Structures: UGC NET CSE | September 2013 | Part 2 | Question: 11

Consider the In-order and Post-order traversals of a tree as given below:

In-order: j e n k o p b f a c l g m d h i

Post-order: j n o p k e f b c l m g h I d a

The Pre-order traversal of the tree shall be



Data Structures: UGC NET CSE | September 2013 | Part 2 | Question: 21
options: a b f e j k n o p c d g l m h i
a b c d e f j k n o p g l m h i
a b e j k n o p f c d g l m h i
j e n o p k f b c l m g h I d a
questions.correct_answer: a b e j k n o p f c d g l m h i
